rings.name: ADOPT
rings.description: We recommend using this for every relevant new project. Proven in production, low risk.

rings.name: TRIAL
rings.description: Worth pursuing. It's important to understand how to build up this capability. Try it on a project that can handle the risk.

rings.name: ASSESS
rings.description: Worth exploring, with the goal of understanding how it will affect your organization. Not yet proven enough to trial.

rings.name: HOLD
rings.description: Proceed with caution. Either avoid for new work, or actively plan to migrate away from it.

entries.title: Design-first OpenAPI
entries.description: Writing the OpenAPI spec before any implementation code, as demonstrated across this lab series, keeps the contract as the single source of truth and lets Backstage catalog the API before a single line of backend code exists.

timeline.description: Adopted from Lab 1 onward for every sample API in this series.

entries.title: Spectral linting in CI
entries.description: Automated OpenAPI/AsyncAPI linting against a shared ruleset (Lab 3's api-grade and Spectral plugins), run continuously rather than only reviewed by eye.

timeline.description: First evaluated alongside Lab 3's api-grade plugin.
Promoted after Lab 3 showed the shared ruleset catching real issues with no false positives worth blocking a merge over.

entries.title: Backstage Software Catalog
entries.description: The catalog itself, as the single place API ownership, visibility, quality, and lifecycle state are recorded and discovered (Labs 1, 2, 6).

timeline.description: Adopted as this series' base platform from Lab 1.

entries.title: Prism mock servers
entries.description: On-demand mock implementations generated directly from an API's own OpenAPI spec (Lab 5), avoiding hand-written stubs that drift from the real contract.

timeline.description: Trialed in Lab 5 as the lazy-loading mock gateway's underlying engine.

entries.title: Self-hosted Backstage
entries.description: Running Backstage locally (this series' approach throughout) keeps the whole lab series at zero cost, with no hosted-platform account required.

timeline.description: The only platform this series uses, by design (Constitution Principle V).

entries.title: AsyncAPI 3.0
entries.description: The event-driven counterpart to OpenAPI, used for this series' Streetlights sample (Lab 1). Still earning full confidence across the plugin ecosystem compared to OpenAPI's maturity.

timeline.description: Assessed alongside Lab 1's AsyncAPI sample; tooling support is improving but younger than OpenAPI's.

entries.title: SOAP/WSDL for new APIs
entries.description: Building brand-new APIs on SOAP/WSDL rather than a modern REST/OpenAPI or AsyncAPI approach. Kept only as a reference point for what this series deliberately avoids (Constitution Principle VII).

timeline.description: Historically trialed industry-wide; not this series' recommendation even at the outset.
Moved to Hold once this series established OpenAPI/AsyncAPI + design-first practices as the taught default (Labs 1-3) — new APIs should not start here.
